name: Neo4j
description: A graph database platform (server, managed AuraDB, drivers, and HTTP/Bolt APIs) for storing and querying graph data with Cypher.

auth_methods.note: The HTTP API documentation explicitly documents Basic Authentication (Authorization: Basic ...) and Bearer tokens (Authorization: Bearer ...). No OAuth2 flow is described on this page; other protocols (e.g., driver auth tokens for Bolt) are covered in driver docs linked from the docs root.

credential_access.note: Aura pricing docs state that an Aura Free tier is available and links to sign-up flows where developers can create an instance and obtain credentials via the Aura Console (see Aura Console docs).

api_surface.note: The docs root lists HTTP/REST API and Bolt protocol (drivers) and links to language drivers (Java, JavaScript, Python, .NET). This indicates both REST HTTP endpoints and driver/bolt APIs are publicly documented and cover broad product functionality (querying, management, etc.).

buildability.note: Public docs exist for HTTP API and drivers and Aura provides a free tier where developers can sign up and obtain credentials via the console, so one can obtain credentials and build against the APIs today.

queries: site:neo4j.com/docs neo4j API documentation developer docs neo4j.com/docs/api neo4j REST HTTP Bolt drivers
site:neo4j.com Aura pricing signup console "Aura Console" "get credentials"
site:neo4j.com "contact sales" "Aura" API "contact sales" neo4j "partner" "API access"
